regions.text: Geometry starts with ideas about lines and spaces. Here are two circles and two squares.
The circle on the left is inside a square. The square on the right is inside a circle. These are relations.
Statements that tally with facts are true. If they don't, they are untrue. To say the right square is outside the circle is a false statement.
fact: something that is true. untrue: not true, false.
